commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b6ce520511e10edd31076a02b8d7ea0362895aad)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram isolated ingress to drain spooled updates through `claimNext`, adds queue selector options, persists or derives lane keys, and updates Telegram and shared queue tests.

PR surface: Source +103, Tests +187. Total +290 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main keeps fresh different-owner claims live-owned when the recorded PID exists, and the linked issue plus duplicates show Telegram pending messages behind a claimed row; this read-only review did not run a live crash reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the blockers are stale-claim scope, SDK API contract, and Telegram proof decisions on a maintainer PR, not a narrow safe automated repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): drain ingress with native queue claims
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #90945.

Telegram isolated ingress stores Bot API updates in the shared SQLite `channel_ingress_events` queue. The bug was that Telegram then drained that queue by listing pending rows and separately claiming a chosen row. If a gateway or handler died after a claim, or if an old claimed row blocked a Telegram lane, later updates could remain stuck even though the durable queue had enough native claim-token machinery to handle this safely.

## Why This Change Was Made

This replaces the gateway startup sweep with a queue-native Telegram drain path. Telegram now asks the shared ingress queue to atomically `claimNext` the next eligible update, using the queue's existing claim token for complete/release/fail operations.

The shared queue gained two narrow `claimNext` options: `orderBy: "id"` so Telegram preserves update-id ordering, and a `deriveLaneKey` callback so legacy Telegram rows that were written before lane keys were persisted can still be skipped when their lane is blocked. New Telegram spool writes persist the lane key up front.

This keeps recovery local to Telegram's drain loop instead of mutating every channel ingress queue during gateway startup.

## User Impact

Telegram users should see stuck isolated-ingress updates drain again without needing manual SQLite edits. Restarted or recovered Telegram sessions can use the existing queue claim flow to skip blocked live lanes, recover eligible stale Telegram claims, and continue unrelated lanes.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts src/channels/message/ingress-queue.ts src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts src/channels/message/ingress-queue.ts src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: romneyda
